title: hieracium pilosella subsp. dasyscapum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. dasyscapum, commonly known as mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a diuretic and to treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Mouse-ear hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
